input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` no longer works in .NET 10 (which would be the next version after .NET 9).

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 9+, the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed because Android itself deprecated the `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` mode. The recommended approach changed.

Let me provide the correct guidance:

In .NET MAUI for .NET 10 (and late .NET 9), the approach shifted. The `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` relied on Android's deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE`. The modern approach is to use `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_PAN` or handle keyboard insets manually.


So the key issue is that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` mapped to Android's `SoftInput.AdjustResize`, which Android deprecated back in API 30. MAUI switched to the newer `WindowInsets` API in .NET 9, so for .NET 10 the best options are using `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Pan` to shift the window up, or leveraging the updated keyboard padding approach with the Android manifest settings.

Actually, I should focus on what MAUI itself provides now—there's `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` that was added specifically to handle keyboard overlap automatically by scrolling content when the keyboard appears. For .NET 10, the simplest approach is wrapping the layout in a `ScrollView` and letting MAUI's built-in keyboard handling take care of it, though there are more granular options if that doesn't work. The practical fix for .NET 10 is to set `android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` in the Android manifest, but since MAUI now uses edge-to-edge rendering, you also need to handle keyboard insets with responsive padding. The simpler alternative is using `AdjustPan` mode, which pans the window up to keep the focused input visible.
